title: polystichum braunii subsp. andersonii
intro: Polystichum braunii subsp. andersonii, also known as the Anderson's fir fern, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the Polystichum braunii species, native to New Zealand. The medicinal properties of this plant are primarily derived from its rhizome, which is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Polystichum braunii subsp. andersonii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of this plant may be made by infusing the rhizome in hot water or using it as a tea, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for medicinal use.
